scenarios.title: logs in with valid credentials and views the full dashboard @positive
scenarios.userAction: Navigate to /login, dismiss the newsletter modal, fill valid credentials, click Sign in, and verify the resulting dashboard shows the URL, nav links, personalised welcome heading, and Logout button.
expectedAssertions: welcome heading contains VALID_USER_DISPLAY_NAME (the user's first name)
Logout button is visible confirming authenticated state

locatorTable.notes: Absolute URL → relative path. baseURL configured in playwright.config.ts via process.env.BASE_URL.
Already on canonical label hierarchy — no change needed. Moved into PageClassLogin as readonly inputEmail field.
Already on canonical label hierarchy — no change needed. Moved into PageClassLogin as readonly inputPassword field.
Button locator is already correct (getByRole). Only { force: true } option is removed. A modal-dismissal step must precede this click. See pin 1 for the modal close locator.
No DOM evidence in source; inferred from inline comment 'Newsletter modal overlay'. Reviewer must verify role and accessible name via Playwright inspector. See pin 1.
Already correct compound role-based locator. Moved into PageClassDashboard as readonly arrayNavLinks field.
Already correct. .toContainText('Jane') assertion updated to .toContainText(VALID_USER_DISPLAY_NAME) constant.
Already correct. Moved into PageClassDashboard as readonly buttonLogout field.

hallucinationDefensePins.sourceLocator: (absent from source — inferred from line 11 comment)
hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q1 unresolved: newsletter modal close locator not confirmed — assumed accessible button with name matching /close|dismiss/i.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Run Playwright inspector (npx playwright test --debug) against /login to capture the modal ARIA tree. If the close control has no accessible name, request that the frontend team add aria-label="Close newsletter" to the button. Fallback locator: page.locator('[data-testid="newsletter-close"]') if a testid exists.

hallucinationDefensePins.assumedLocator: modal always shown on fresh BrowserContext visit to /login
hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q2 unresolved: modal appearance conditions not confirmed — assumed always-present on first page load in a fresh context.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: If modal is cookie-gated: add page.addInitScript() in base.fixture.ts to set the newsletter opt-in cookie before navigation, preventing the modal from appearing and making the dismissal step unnecessary. Document the cookie name.
